hypothesis: Incorporating protein flexibility through ensemble pharmacophore models derived from multiple receptor conformations (e.g., molecular dynamics snapshots of the alpha estrogen receptor) as the reference set for CATS descriptor computation will yield generated molecules with superior experimental binding affinities compared to pharmacophore models derived from a single crystallographic structure, because ensemble-based pharmacophores capture the dynamic interaction landscape of the binding site and thereby provide a more biologically accurate optimization signal for the reinforcement learning reward function.
